TextENGNew: Spring, Showa 56 -- Somewhere
changeReason: Date format changed from Western calendar year to Japanese Showa era dating, which alters how the reader contextualizes the time period.

TextENGNew: ...When was the last time food and drink had tasted this wonderful?
On this day, Tomoe was savoring what was probably the happiest moment of her life -- or at the very least, in the top three.
changeReason: Old translation says definitively 'third happiest'; new translation says 'the happiest -- or at the very least, in the top three,' matching the Japanese 一番か、少なくとも三番目以上 (the best, or at least third or higher).

TextENGNew: "...Come on, you've had too much to drink already.@k@vS08/29/321200110. Sis, seriously."
"Ahahahaha.@k@vS08/28/320500646. It's fine every once in a while, isn't it?@k@vS08/28/320500647. Ah, excuuuse me~"

Perhaps a bit tipsy, Tomoe laughed in high spirits as she slid open the fusuma and called for the waitress. She ordered two more bottles of beer and asked if they could also drink the wine she'd brought specially for the occasion.
"Please.@k@vS08/28/320500649. Just for today, could you make an exception?"
changeReason: Old translation opens with 'I'm sorry' but Japanese お願いします means 'Please'; new translation corrects the speech act.

TextENGNew: "Well, I suppose..."
changeReason: Old translation uses 'of course' (certainty), new uses 'I suppose' (hesitant agreement); the Japanese そうですねぇ expresses tentative agreement, making the new version more accurate.

TextENGNew: Strictly speaking, bringing outside food and drink into this establishment was prohibited.
...However, when Tomoe explained the circumstances, the waitress shrugged with a wry smile, said 'Our little secret,' retreated to the kitchen, and returned with two elegant wine glasses.
"Yes, this is it!@k@vS08/28/320500651. Wine just doesn't taste right out of a regular cup, you know~@k@vS08/28/320500652. Well then, now that we have permission, let's polish off the whole bottle!"

"Honestly, you're so pushy...@k@vS08/29/321200112. And isn't that wine terribly expensive?@k@vS08/29/321200113. I think it was a Chateau... something or other.@k@vS08/29/321200114. Are you really sure about this?"

"What are you talking about?@k@vS08/28/320500654. Today is your celebration for being accepted into the police force, remember?@k@vS08/28/320500655. I was actually thinking of getting something even pricier, but this will have to do for now."
"This is already plenty extravagant..."
Madoka shook her head in exasperation. ...But even so, she couldn't hide the happiness on her face.
Indeed. Tomoe was unusually giddy that evening. Under normal circumstances, she would never dream of breaking a restaurant's rules.
But it was understandable. After that fight long ago that had driven them apart, her sister had finally resolved to take up honest work...
...Back when their parents died, and it was just the two of them.
Tomoe had vowed to protect her sister -- still bearing the psychological scars from the aftermath of the incident -- for the rest of her life.
To that end, she gave up track and field, which had been her greatest source of pride, and declined the athletic scholarship to a sports university.
changeReason: Changed 'personal invitation' to 'athletic scholarship'; 推薦入学 refers to an admission by recommendation/scholarship, not a personal invitation.

TextENGNew: "Minai... are you really sure about this?@k@vS08/33/VT2B_tsukada1002. With your current ability, you could aim for a spot on the Japanese team in the next World Athletics Championship.@k@vS08/33/VT2B_tsukada1003. If university doesn't work, I could write you a recommendation for a corporate team."
"So you don't have to give up on track entirely, do you...?"
Her teammates, the alumni, the faculty advisor... every one of them had said so, trying to stop her.
changeReason: Changed 'counselor' to 'faculty advisor'; 顧問 specifically means a club advisor/supervisor, not a guidance counselor.

TextENGNew: Even so, Tomoe's resolve did not waver in the slightest.

"As long as I continue with track, I'll inevitably be consumed by practice and competitions every day.@k@vS08/28/320500657. Until my sister regains the brightness she once had, I want to do everything I possibly can for her."
changeReason: Changed 'exercise' to 'competitions'; 遠征 means away games/competitions/expeditions, not exercise.

TextENGNew: Yes. For Tomoe, her sister was far more important than her own extracurriculars or career path.
From then on, Tomoe threw herself into filling the void left by their absent parents.
During Madoka's hospitalization, she provided emotional support, stayed by her side through rehabilitation, and helped her catch up on the schoolwork she'd missed.
changeReason: Old translation says Tomoe 'skipped out on school'; new translation describes her staying by Madoka's side through rehabilitation and helping with missed schoolwork, which better captures the Japanese つきっきりでリハビリと休学中の補習に付き合って.

TextENGNew: After that was over and they returned home, she studied for her own entrance exams until the early hours of dawn to make up for lost time.
changeReason: Changed 'studied day and night' to 'until the early hours of dawn'; 明け方近くまで specifically means 'until nearly dawn,' indicating late-night study rather than all-day study.

TextENGNew: When Madoka was discharged and returned to school, Tomoe shielded her whenever she got into trouble -- which happened time and again -- bowing her head to others in apology.
changeReason: New translation adds detail about 'bowing her head to others in apology' (頭を下げて回った), which was omitted in the original translation.

TextENGNew: Desperately believing that all of it would help them reclaim even a little of their happiness...
changeReason: New translation changes 'her former happiness' to 'their happiness' and 'believing' to 'desperately believing'; 懸命に means desperately, and 自分たちの means 'their (own)' referring to both sisters.

TextENGNew: That said, between the short preparation period and the disadvantage of her study environment, Tomoe had been on the verge of giving up on the university entrance exam.
And yet -- whether through fortune or the fruit of her efforts -- she somehow managed to pass the entrance exam for a national university in Ibaraki.
She chose Ibaraki, where their grandmother lived, over familiar Tokyo -- needless to say, for Madoka's sake, as school had become increasingly difficult for her.
Once Tomoe started university, there were relatives who helped support the orphaned sisters with tuition and living expenses.
...But Tomoe saved every last yen of it for Madoka's future, never touching a cent for herself.
"It's easier to find part-time work at a national university, and there are scholarships available.@k@vS08/28/320500659. ...I'd like to set this money aside as a marriage fund for Madoka."
There was no falsehood in those words she'd spoken to their worried grandmother.
Tomoe believed that the probability of making one person happy was far greater than trying to make both of them happy.
But contrary to her devotion... Madoka abruptly dropped out of high school one day.
And when Tomoe hurried home to confront her about it, their grandmother informed her that Madoka had already left...
"I'm sorry.@k@vS08/29/321200117. I don't want to be your burden anymore.@k@vS08/29/321200118. I don't want to cause you any more trouble, Sis."
Only that letter remained in her room......
"Please don't come looking for me.@k@vS08/29/321200120. ...Take care. Goodbye..."
"What... what is this?!@k@vS08/28/320500661. A burden...? Who said anything about a burden...?!"
Who said such a thing?! When did she ever think that?! Not for a single instant had Tomoe ever considered Madoka's existence a burden!!
She had always been my precious... my one and only little sister...!!
"You idiot... Madoka, you absolute idiot!!"
She searched frantically.
She visited every place she could think of, called every acquaintance one after another.
Walking late into the night, day after day. Walking, walking, never stopping...
And after wearing through several pairs of shoes, all she learned for certain... was the cold, hard fact that Madoka was no longer in her city.
The night she learned that Madoka had boarded a bullet train with farewell money from friends... Tomoe stood alone in a rain-drenched alley... looked up to the heavens, and wept.
She had desperately held back her grief even through the death of their parents, but... that night, she was powerless to stop the tears from flowing.
If she hadn't happened to meet Ooishi at that moment... Tomoe might well have destroyed herself in her despair.
...Then, several years later.
While on assignment in Tokyo as a police officer, Tomoe -- by sheer coincidence -- ran into Madoka on a downtown street corner.
And when she discovered that Madoka was working in a disreputable establishment... for the first time in her life, she struck her.
"...No!@k@vS08/29/321200122. I'm not going back!!"
Her cheek reddened, Madoka batted away Tomoe's outstretched hand and said that.
"Leave me alone!@k@vS08/29/321200124. I may not be as smart as you, Sis, but I'm living on my own terms, thinking things through in my own way!"
"Thinking things through?@k@vS08/28/320500666. What things, exactly?!@k@vS08/28/320500667. Your idea of thinking is plying drinks and spinning lies for middle-aged men?!"

"It brings in money, which makes it a proper job!@k@vS08/29/321200126. Everyone here is working seriously and desperately hard!@k@vS08/29/321200127. Don't look down on us with your narrow-minded prejudices!!"
The pain in her fists that day... she still hadn't forgotten it.
And several more years had passed since then, up to the present.
Since that day... Tomoe had been prepared for the possibility that she might never see her sister again.
Even so, when Madoka suddenly reached out after all this time... and Tomoe had traveled all this way to be here -- it meant that deep down, she had been hoping for this moment.
(A lot happened between us, but... it doesn't matter anymore.@k@vS08/28/320500671. Madoka has finally had a change of heart. I couldn't ask for more...)
What's more, Madoka intended to become a police officer -- just like Tomoe and their father. ...Tomoe was deeply, profoundly moved.

"Come on, your chopsticks have stopped.@k@vS08/28/320500673. What are you having?@k@vS08/28/320500674. Oh, they have shiokara! Let's order that.@k@vS08/28/320500675. You used to love that, didn't you?"

"E-enough already...@k@vS08/29/321200129. Since when does my frugal sister have such a change of heart?@k@vS08/29/321200130. I mean, I did say we should celebrate, but we didn't have to go to such a fancy restaurant..."

"What are you talking about?@k@vS08/28/320500677. ...Listen, Madoka.@k@vS08/28/320500678. This is exactly the kind of occasion money is for.@k@vS08/28/320500679. Being wasteful is one thing, but being stingy all the time doesn't make life any fun, does it?@k@vS08/28/320500680. Am I wrong?"
"Wow, I can't believe you of all people are saying that..."
"Oh, hush.@k@vS08/28/320500682. Besides, starting the month after next, I'll be a deputy inspector.@k@vS08/28/320500683. My salary is going up too, so leave this to me."
"Oh. Right, you're finally making deputy inspector.@k@vS08/29/321200133. Bit late to say it, but congratulations."
"Yeah, thanks."
They clinked their glasses, brimming with amber wine, and toasted each other. A few weeks earlier, Tomoe had officially received her promotion orders to deputy inspector.
Even as a university graduate, it was virtually unheard of for a woman to rise to deputy inspector at this speed through the non-career track.
Of course, Tomoe attributed it largely to the halo of her father's distinguished name happening to work in her favor, and harbored little sense of being elite...
"Oh, but since you're in the deputy inspector training period now, you're living in the academy dormitory, right?@k@vS08/29/321200135. Going out this late at night -- will you make curfew?"

"It's fine.@k@vS08/28/320500686. Unlike the initial training, they're fairly relaxed about that sort of thing.@k@vS08/28/320500687. Today's Saturday, so as long as I take the bullet train back tomorrow, there won't be a problem."
"Wow~@k@vS08/29/321200137. Hey, what's dormitory life like?@k@vS08/29/321200138. Tell me, for reference."
"Sure thing.@k@vS08/28/320500689. You'll be entering the police academy next month too, after all.@k@vS08/28/320500690. Let's see..."
Saying that, Tomoe pulled from her bag the old textbooks and pamphlets she'd brought along.
True to the diligent sister's character, every page was worn and battered, and opening them revealed a sea of highlighter marks and sticky notes.
(She must have dug these out from the back of a closet somewhere...)
She could picture her sister covered in dust, turning the whole house upside down in hopes of finding something useful for her.
No matter the occasion, Tomoe was the kind of person who would pour her heart into anything for Madoka's sake.
And despite the years of estrangement between them, she hadn't changed one bit...
...Pressing a finger firmly against the corner of her eye as it threatened to well up, Madoka forced a cheerful expression and leaned forward.
It would be terribly boorish to dampen a celebration like this.
"Wow, there's a lot to memorize.@k@vS08/29/321200142. Constitutional law, civil law, criminal law...@k@vS08/29/321200143. Whoa, English is mandatory?"
"Of course it is. Foreign nationals are proper citizens too.@k@vS08/28/320500692. You'll be in for a rude awakening if you take it lightly."
"Oh no...@k@vS08/29/321200145. I never thought I'd have to wrestle with a foreign language at this age..."
"...What era are you from?@k@vS08/28/320500694. Well, you did take after Dad and his terrible English handwriting, didn't you~?"
"How rude.@k@vS08/29/321200147. I was never as bad as Dad.@k@vS08/29/321200148. ...Probably."

"Ahahahaha.@k@vS08/28/320500696. On top of that, there's traffic law, first aid procedures, and more.@k@vS08/28/320500697. And of course, arrest techniques and martial arts.@k@vS08/28/320500698. ...Come to think of it, Madoka, didn't you do kendo before?"
"'Before'... that was back in elementary school.@k@vS08/29/321200150. I quit right after getting first kyu, so it's basically as if I never did it."
"You'll be fine -- your body will remember.@k@vS08/28/320500700. It was the same for me."
"Don't lump me in with you.@k@vS08/29/321200152. I mean, honestly~..."
And for a while, the two of them swapped stories from the past, weaving their memories together.
There was no shortage of topics -- the conversation and the laughter never ran dry...
Along with the nostalgia... there was a joy in reclaiming the time they had lost.
"By the way, are there other women at the academy besides you, Sis?"

"Quite a few, actually.@k@vS08/28/320500702. Female officer recruitment has been increasing lately.@k@vS08/28/320500703. ...That said, I seem to be the only deputy inspector candidate this year.@k@vS08/28/320500704. So when I mention that, everyone's surprised."
"Well, of course they are.@k@vS08/29/321200155. A woman under thirty making deputy inspector?@k@vS08/29/321200156. That's unheard of."
...Society was loudly championing 'gender equality' across various fields. But putting that into practice in the workplace was another matter entirely.
Police officers, in particular, were expected to possess the physical fortitude to confront all manner of criminals, which inevitably made being a 'woman' a perceived disadvantage.
In reality, since promotions depended primarily on one's superior's recommendation and a panel interview rather than ability alone, Tomoe had come to terms with this.
...Which was why, when she learned she had passed on her first attempt, she could scarcely believe her ears.
"The higher-ups these days seem rather open-minded.@k@vS08/28/320500706. I never expected to reach Dad's rank this quickly..."
Saying that, Tomoe drained the last of her wine in one gulp. Madoka smiled ruefully as she refilled both their empty glasses to the brim.

Tomoe had graduated from a national university, even if a regional one, while their father, Yuusuke Minai, had been a high school graduate. The difference in their starting points made a simple comparison impossible.
But for the two of them... the title of 'deputy inspector' held a special significance.
"...Sis.@k@vS08/29/321200159. Are you still chasing... that 'thing' Dad left behind?"
"Huh?@k@vS08/28/320500708. Yeah, of course."
'That thing' referred to a memo of their father's that Tomoe had discovered by chance in the scorched remains of a storage shed after their parents died in the fire.
According to the memo, their father, Yuusuke Minai, appeared to have discovered something related to a Wednesday in June. But what it actually meant remained unsolved to this day...
"Once you're deputy inspector, you'll have access to more past investigation files than before, right?@k@vS08/29/321200161. That's great -- things should be much easier going forward."

"Yeah.@k@vS08/28/320500710. ...But becoming deputy inspector also means being busier than ever, so I won't be able to take much time off.@k@vS08/28/320500711. On top of that, the person at the NPA who'd been helping me is apparently being transferred soon, so..."
"I see.@k@vS08/29/321200163. It's been almost... ten years since then."
Had ten years been long, or had they passed in an instant? ...Even Tomoe wasn't sure.
But since losing her parents and being separated from Madoka, that pursuit had been her entire reason for living... and the years she had devoted to it were far too heavy to dismiss as wasted effort.
"...Ah, ahahaha.@k@vS08/28/320500714. Sorry for bringing up something like that when we're supposed to be celebrating.@k@vS08/28/320500715. Come on, let's just drink tonight!"

"Honestly, you don't have to push yourself so hard...@k@vS08/29/321200165. You're already a big eater and a heavy drinker as it is, Sis.@k@vS08/29/321200166. Cutting back a little would just bring you to normal."
"How rude~@k@vS08/28/320500717. I don't actually drink that often.@k@vS08/28/320500718. It's just that when I do, I go all out."
"That's what they call a heavy drinker."
"Nyahahahaha!@k@vS08/28/320500720. ...Oh, that's right.@k@vS08/28/320500721. Since I'm here, mind if I stay at your place tonight?"
"Yeah, that'd be perfect.@k@vS08/28/320500723. I'd save on hotel costs too.@k@vS08/28/320500724. That's okay, right, Madoka?"

"See, that's exactly the kind of thing that makes you cheap... @k@vS08/29/321200170. Wait, that's not the point.@k@vS08/29/321200171. You want to come to my place?@k@vS08/29/321200172. Um, that's a little..."
"Oh come on, we're sisters.@k@vS08/28/320500726. It's not like you're living with someone, right?"
At Madoka's reaction, Tomoe stopped mid-pour and looked up. ...Madoka's face had turned slightly red, and her eyes were darting about as if struggling with something.
"...Is there someone?@k@vS08/28/320500729. Someone you're living with...?"
"Huh?@k@vS08/29/321200175. Well, um...@k@vS08/29/321200176. ...t-technically, I'm the one living in their place."
Slightly taken aback, Tomoe felt her drunkenness recede.
Come to think of it, they were both grown women now. It shouldn't have been surprising.
But... realizing that her sister was no longer the girl from those days -- when Tomoe had been her only ally in the world -- made her feel lonely.
"S-sorry?@k@vS08/29/321200178. I wasn't trying to keep it a secret.@k@vS08/29/321200179. I just... wanted to wait until things were a bit more settled before telling you..."
"What are you saying?@k@vS08/28/320500733. Living together is already practically a done deal."
"No, Sis, that's a pretty old-fashioned way of looking at it..."
...Tomoe had always been remarkably 'innocent' when it came to matters of romance.

It was probably past the statute of limitations by now, but if it ever came out that Madoka had crashed at a certain man's place when she first ran away from home, Tomoe would undoubtedly launch into a lecture lasting until dawn...
"Huh?"
"What kind of person is he?@k@vS08/28/320500736. ...If you tell me he's someone from the nightlife industry, I'm going to hit you."
"I-it's not like that!@k@vS08/29/321200183. He has a proper title and social standing.@k@vS08/29/321200184. He's also very kind, and earnest..."
"I can't possibly know that without meeting him.@k@vS08/28/320500738. So you'll introduce us soon.@k@vS08/28/320500739. That's an order, by the way."
"...Come on, don't pull the big sister card like that.@k@vS08/29/321200186. Besides, you already know him.@k@vS08/29/321200187. You know -- the one at the NPA who's been helping..."
"At the NPA, who's been... helping?@k@vS08/28/320500741. Hmm..."
Hearing that, Tomoe tried to recall the faces of the people she'd met at the National Police Agency.
...But most of them were married. Which meant...
"Don't tell me... an affair?!"
"Excuse me.@k@vS08/29/321200189. What exactly do you think of me?@k@vS08/29/321200190. ...Of course not.@k@vS08/29/321200191. We're properly considering marriage, okay?"
"Then... who is it?@k@vS08/28/320500744. You can tell me, can't you?"
"Fine, I'll tell you... but don't be shocked, okay?"
"...Wh-whaaaaaat?!"
"Y-you're joking!!@k@vS08/28/320500747. I absolutely will not accept this!!!"
"...Inspector?@k@vS08/31/320900027. Inspector Minai..."
"-- Ugh, be quiet!@k@vS08/28/320500749. I'm in the middle of something!!@k@vS08/28/320500750. And I'm not an inspector -- I'm a deputy inspector!!"
"Please wake up, Inspector Minai..."
July 7th, Showa 58 (Thursday) -- Inside Kakiuchi Police Station
changeReason: Date format changed from Western calendar year to Japanese Showa era dating, which alters how the reader contextualizes the time period.

TextENGNew: She opened her eyes... and found a familiar face right above her.
"Fujita-kun...?"
"Sorry to disturb your rest.@k@vS08/31/320900030. ...May I have a moment?"
Stifling a small yawn as she sat up, Tomoe looked around through half-lidded eyes.
She was in the break room. She'd meant to rest on one of the long benches for just a bit in the middle of the night... and had apparently fallen fast asleep.
Through the window came the pale light of dawn and the chirping of sparrows. ...Someone had apparently covered her -- a large blanket lay at her feet.
"A dream, huh..."
"...Pardon?"
"No, it's nothing."
Her consciousness finally clear, Tomoe straightened up on the bench and briskly folded the blanket. She gave both cheeks a sharp slap to wake herself up, then turned to face Detective Fujita.
"...Has something come up?"

"Yes, regarding the ongoing matter.@k@vS08/31/320900033. I'm sorry for the early hour, but could you come to Conference Room 3?@k@vS08/31/320900034. Detective Ooishi and Inspector Akasaka should be arriving shortly as well."
"Understood.@k@vS08/28/320500759. ...Oh, I'd like to grab a quick shower first. Can you give me ten minutes?"
"Of course. I'll see you there."
Detective Fujita gave a solemn bow and left the break room.
He was the straightforward type, and his emotions showed easily on his face. ...From the look of him, it wasn't hard to guess that the news wouldn't be good.
"...Right, then."
Tomoe stood and left the break room. As she walked down the still-empty corridor toward the shower room in the nap quarters... she found herself mulling over the dream she'd had.
(We ended up fighting and parting ways again that day, didn't we...)

After the celebration, she'd boarded the last bullet train with her head boiling in anger... drank herself senseless on the way... and got shaken awake by the conductor at Tokyo Station... or so she vaguely recalled.
She'd been so thoroughly drunk that she remembered almost nothing.
...And to top it off, she woke up on a park bench. She was amazed she'd come through in one piece.
Fortunately, Madoka called a few days later to say 'Sorry about the other day,' which spared them another complete estrangement...
(Looking back on it now... there really was no need to fly off the handle like that...)
Her sister... and the man from the NPA who had helped her time and again -- their father's former superior, and her own current boss.
...Love had no age limit. If this was the partner Madoka had chosen with serious intent, she should accept it.
The age gap was admittedly a lingering source of personal discomfort, but it was no grounds for strong opposition.
What she'd felt that night had been nothing more than jealousy. ...A loneliness akin to a parent's, watching her sister drift beyond her reach.
"...That said."
Tomoe recalled what had happened a few days ago. The words she herself had spoken to Natsumi.
That you shouldn't merely wait for someone to believe in you -- you should make the effort to believe.
If the person who said it wasn't practicing it herself, then no matter how fine the words sounded, they were nothing but empty theory...
"That's right.@k@vS08/28/320500765. I made such a grand declaration to that girl,@k@vS08/28/320500766. so maybe it's about time..."
Murmuring to herself, Tomoe nodded -- to no one in particular -- and lifted her face.
The heaviness of sleep seemed to lighten, just a little.
"-- All right then, Hanada-kun, let's hear the report."
"Yes.@k@vS08/32/VT2B_hanada1010. ...It's about the Kimiyoshi family's grandmother, Aki.@k@vS08/32/VT2B_hanada1011. As expected, no one has sighted her for the past several days."
Detective Hanada pointed to a photograph of an elderly woman pinned to the whiteboard with a magnet as he read aloud from his report.
Under Inspector Minai's orders, he had been conducting inquiries at the places Aki Kimiyoshi was known to frequent.
"So you're saying she's shut herself in at home...?"
"No, and that's what's strange.@k@vS08/32/VT2B_hanada1013. Actually, a delivery driver visited the Kimiyoshi house the day before yesterday and said the inside was eerily, unnaturally silent."
That same delivery driver had apparently visited the Kimiyoshi house a short while before as well... back when it had been adorned with those unsettling occult decorations.
At that time, he had clearly heard the old woman chanting some sort of strange sutras in a low voice from a room deep in the house.
"So she's stopped performing her rituals, then?@k@vS08/11/321100554. Or perhaps she's taken ill..."
"There are... other possibilities we might want to consider as well."
"? ...Such as?"
Detective Hanada glanced toward Inspector Minai, looking slightly uncertain. ...She caught his gaze and nodded, motioning for him to 'Go on.'

"Actually... on Saturday night.@k@vS08/32/VT2B_hanada1016. A student studying for exams in the house two doors from the Kimiyoshi residence heard the sound of a car engine.@k@vS08/32/VT2B_hanada1017. Based on what was playing on the late-night radio at the time... it was estimated to be around 2 AM."

"Touji Kimiyoshi's car is kept in a parking lot some distance away.@k@vS08/32/VT2B_hanada1019. ...However, we have testimony that he took it through a car wash at a nearby gas station last month after returning from a visit to Hinamizawa."
"Despite that, when I examined the car, the underside of the body near the tires was caked with grass-flecked mud."
"Oh?@k@vS08/11/321100556. Which means... he must have driven somewhere with unpaved mountain roads recently.@k@vS08/11/321100557. ...Nfu fu fu!"
Ooishi-san chuckled with a fearless grin as he looked over the documents before him.
Just as Detective Hanada had described, the evidence photographs clipped to the files showed clear traces of dried mud.

"I then conducted a voluntary interview with Touji Kimiyoshi.@k@vS08/32/VT2B_hanada1022. He readily admitted to traveling to the mountains of Gifu Prefecture on Sunday.@k@vS08/32/VT2B_hanada1023. He claimed he went stream fishing."
"Stream fishing, you say...?"

"When I guided the conversation to let me see inside his car trunk,@k@vS08/32/VT2B_hanada1025. I did indeed find a full set of fishing gear and the lingering smell of fish, just as he'd described.@k@vS08/32/VT2B_hanada1026. ...However."
"However...?"
"Just to be thorough, I checked with some of his coworkers, and none of them had ever heard that fishing was a hobby of his."
"Furthermore, I personally examined the lures in his tackle...@k@vS08/32/VT2B_hanada1029. and they were clearly designed for ocean fishing."
Saying that, Detective Hanada placed two lures on the desk.
...There wasn't a dramatic difference at first glance, but the one he indicated as 'for stream fishing' was noticeably smaller in construction.

"If I may bring up a personal matter -- I happen to be an avid fisherman myself.@k@vS08/32/VT2B_hanada1031. Even a rank beginner wouldn't confuse these -- and to claim he actually caught something with the wrong equipment is quite hard to believe."
"Oh?@k@vS08/11/321100560. Incidentally, Detective Hanada, are you any good at fishing?"
"Fairly decent, I'd say.@k@vS08/32/VT2B_hanada1033. My wife is partial to river fish, and her mood depends entirely on my results, so I've done quite a bit of research over the years."
"All right, that's enough of the love story.@k@vS08/28/320500770. ...The bottom line is that there's a high probability of fabrication.@k@vS08/28/320500771. Correct, Hanada-kun?"
"Yes, that's correct."
"I see..."
"...This is bad, Ooishi-san."
Aki Kimiyoshi's 'absence,' the late-night 'engine sound,' the 'evidence of a trip to the mountains,' and the 'fabrication.'
If the conclusion one could draw from all of this was true... then we were already too late.
"Should we press Touji Kimiyoshi harder?"
"No, I think it's too early for that.@k@vS08/32/VT2B_hanada1036. She's reportedly reclusive by nature, so if he simply insists she's home, we'll have no grounds to push further."
"And even if we tried to obtain a search warrant, we don't have definitive evidence yet..."
"Quite so.@k@vS08/11/321100563. Besides, it's possible he isn't the principal but merely an accomplice.@k@vS08/11/321100564. In which case, there's a risk the principal might 'silence' him."
Inspector Minai buried her chin in her clasped hands and closed her eyes briefly. Then, as if having gathered her thoughts, she opened them again and looked up at Detective Hanada.
"We'll let Touji Kimiyoshi swim for now.@k@vS08/28/320500774. Instead, we'll dispatch investigators to the mountain he claims to have gone fishing in."

"The justification will be a search for Aki Kimiyoshi, who is suspected missing.@k@vS08/28/320500776. Hanada-kun, speak with Section Chief Yamamura and see if we can borrow some personnel from Division One."
"Understood."

"Have the station chief contact Gifu Prefectural Police and Sawaguchi Station to secure their cooperation.@k@vS08/28/320500778. Also, contact prefectural headquarters and arrange for a forensics team."
"It will take time to get things moving, but considering the implications, it can't be helped."
"Roger."
Detective Hanada rose from his seat and hurried out of the room.
"Shall we help as well?@k@vS08/13/321000678. For a mountain search, more people would surely be better."
"Ah, no.@k@vS08/28/320500781. ...Inspector Akasaka, Ooishi-san -- there's something else I'd like to ask you both to handle urgently, if you're willing."
"Something else? ...What would that be?"
"This."
Saying that, Inspector Minai produced a palm-sized pink case from her inside pocket. Opening the lid revealed packages of capsules in an unusual color.
"...What is this?"
"Natsumi Kimiyoshi was carrying it on her person.@k@vS08/28/320500784. I'd meant to return it, but I kept missing the opportunity until now..."
"Hmm. Cold medicine or something?@k@vS08/11/321100568. ...These capsules are a rather unappetizing color."
"I contacted the major manufacturers using the printed markings, but none of them matched.@k@vS08/28/320500786. There's a possibility these are unregulated drugs."
Normally, prescription medications bear printed codes indicating the drug name and manufacturer.
If no manufacturer claimed them, it meant they were unregulated -- and their domestic use should be strictly controlled.
"I've asked Matsuoka-kun at Gifu Prefectural Police to analyze the drug's composition.@k@vS08/28/320500788. He's skilled at this sort of analysis."
"...And I'd like the two of you to go to Saeki General Hospital and check Natsumi Kimiyoshi's treatment history."
"I see.@k@vS08/13/321000680. You think this medication was prescribed there?"

"I can't say for certain yet.@k@vS08/28/320500791. ...However, Saeki General Hospital is the only major hospital in the area where she lives.@k@vS08/28/320500792. So it seems like the most likely place she could have obtained this."
"That's true.@k@vS08/11/321100570. It's hard to imagine a small clinic handling unregulated medication, given the costs and risks involved."
"Also, about this drug.@k@vS08/28/320500794. ...The shape and color closely resemble the capsule we found at the Hatakeyama residence.@k@vS08/28/320500795. Please take a look."
Sure enough, the photograph Inspector Minai pointed to showed the capsule she had collected the other day.
"Oh?@k@vS08/11/321100572. ...Now that you mention it, they do look similar.@k@vS08/11/321100573. I'd thought it was an oddly flashy color for medication."
"It could be a mere coincidence, but just to be safe, it would be helpful if you could also look into the treatment histories of the entire Hatakeyama family in addition to Natsumi Kimiyoshi's."
"The search warrant has been obtained, so please identify yourselves as detectives 'this time.'"
"...Ha, haha.@k@vS08/13/321000682. We appreciate the consideration."
That was surely a jab at our earlier blunder -- when our cover story about being publishers fell apart. ...She was more one to hold a grudge than I'd expected.
"I'd originally intended to handle this myself, but I need to file the report on that arson-homicide case by today...@k@vS08/28/320500799. Would you be willing to take this on?"
"Of course.@k@vS08/13/321000684. Let's head out right away, Ooishi-san."
"Indeed.@k@vS08/11/321100575. Well then, Tomoe-chan, look forward to some good news today -- consider it a congratulatory gift.@k@vS08/11/321100576. ...Nfu fu fu!"
"A congratulatory gift?@k@vS08/28/320500801. I haven't the faintest idea what you mean..."
I couldn't help but chuckle at the look of bewilderment on Inspector Minai's face.
In any case... Saeki General Hospital. I could only hope we'd find some useful information there...
"...All right then, Fujita-kun, let's hear your report."
After Akasaka and Ooishi left the room, Tomoe's expression shifted completely as she turned to face Fujita.
...He hadn't uttered a single word until now. He produced a report he'd hidden beneath the desk and handed it to Tomoe, his expression grave.
"What?"
"Is this all right?@k@vS08/31/320900038. Not informing Hanada, Inspector Akasaka, and the others about this."
"...I didn't want them to hear it.@k@vS08/28/320500805. Those two... not yet."
Tomoe answered with a sigh as she flipped open the cover of the clipped report.

"Hanada-kun just had a baby daughter, didn't he?@k@vS08/28/320500807. And Inspector Akasaka has a daughter too.@k@vS08/28/320500808. ...I didn't think it wise to share this casually when nothing's been confirmed yet."
"But... I believe it's all but confirmed at this point."
Then Tomoe turned the page and examined the forensic photographs.
Fujita had borrowed them from the forensics team. They showed the interior of the Hatakeyama household immediately after the incident -- specifically, the room of the eldest son, Kentarou.
It must have been fired from close range... enormous bloodstains were smeared across the wall, as if from an explosion.

Unlike when they had visited the day before, the raw immediacy of the scene was palpable even through photographs. ...Tomoe had thought she was used to this by now, but the murky color of the blood made her wince involuntarily.
"I... still can't believe it.@k@vS08/31/320900041. Honestly, this time I was genuinely hoping your theory was wrong, Minai-san."
"I see.@k@vS08/28/320500811. ...Actually, I feel the same.@k@vS08/28/320500812. Even now, I'm still hoping it was just my overactive imagination.@k@vS08/28/320500813. ...But we have to face reality."
Saying that, Tomoe arranged the photographs on the desk according to the marked tabs.
From the collection, she pulled two to the front: one of the wall in Kentarou's room that she had photographed earlier, and another of the wall in the bedroom shared by his parents, the Masao couple.
"...The first thing that struck me as odd was the way this blood was scattered.@k@vS08/28/320500815. And after going to see it firsthand, 'maybe' became 'probably.'"
What Tomoe pointed to was the bloodstain on the wall of Kentarou's room.
"When you shoulder a rifle to fire... you normally aim from about shoulder height.@k@vS08/28/320500817. So the bullet trajectory should correspond to the shooter's height."
"...Right."

"But it's different when you're shooting a shorter target at close range, isn't it?@k@vS08/28/320500819. Most adults would stand and simply angle the muzzle downward before pulling the trigger.@k@vS08/28/320500820. In that case --"
Naturally, the bullet would strike downward into Kentarou's head...

"Kentarou was shot in the head at close range and died instantly.@k@vS08/28/320500822. ...If that's the case, it's strange for the bloodstain to be on the 'wall.'@k@vS08/28/320500823. If fired from a downward angle, the blood should have spread across the 'floor.'"
"But the bloodstains and the bullet were both found on the wall...@k@vS08/28/320500825. which means the rifle was held at roughly Kentarou's height..."
"Yes.@k@vS08/31/320900044. ...The forensics team reached the same conclusion..."

"And there's another thing.@k@vS08/28/320500827. The theory is that Masao committed suicide by pressing the rifle against his own throat.@k@vS08/28/320500828. ...But considering the angle of the rifle, there's an alternative interpretation."
"Namely... a 'perpetrator' shorter than Masao fired the rifle from a low, upward angle --"
"...@k@vS08/31/320900046. Then you're saying... the real culprit... is 'her'...?"
"Yes.@k@vS08/28/320500831. ...At this point, the primary suspect is...@k@vS08/28/320500832. ...the eldest daughter, Aoi Hatakeyama."
As she drafted the final report on the nurse arson-homicide case from her notes... Tomoe's mind kept drifting back to her earlier exchange with Fujita.
She was used to seeing him indignant at injustice and dejected... but this time was especially painful to watch.
"...But even so, Aoi Hatakeyama is only ten years old.@k@vS08/31/320900048. For a child that small to handle an enormous weapon like a rifle... it's unthinkable!"
"She just can't get a license, that's all.@k@vS08/28/320500835. The operation itself is simple enough for a child."
He tried to form a rebuttal... but the words wouldn't come.
And with good reason -- accidental hunting rifle discharges occurred several times a year nationwide, based on reported cases alone.
Among them were worst-case incidents where children had pulled the trigger half in play, killing or injuring someone.
So it wasn't so extraordinary as to be dismissed as 'unthinkable.'
Moreover, Tomoe had previously obtained testimony from neighboring housewives that Aoi's father, Masao Hatakeyama, had taken her along on his hunting trips more than once.
"This is only speculation.@k@vS08/28/320500837. ...But what if Masao, thinking it was fun, had taught his daughter how to shoot?"
If she'd learned the operation and shown interest, if she'd asked to try it herself and he'd let her -- then the assumption that 'a girl couldn't have done it' would be thoroughly overturned...

"And look at this photograph.@k@vS08/28/320500839. Even with a shotgun, the recoil would be brutal on a child's body.@k@vS08/28/320500840. ...So if she braced her back against the wall and fired like this --"
After demonstrating the posture against the conference room wall, Tomoe picked up a photograph she'd taken herself, showing the closet's sliding door.
On the opposite side of that door was the wall where Yoshiko's blood had splattered.
...And corroborating her theory, the sliding door bore an indentation about one meter from the floor, as though something hard had been driven into it.
"So when Aoi Hatakeyama claimed she was struck on the head and lost consciousness..."
"...That was most likely a lie.@k@vS08/28/320500842. When she fired the final shot at Masao, unlike with Kentarou and Yoshiko, Aoi couldn't absorb the recoil."
"It was probably because she fired with the muzzle angled upward.@k@vS08/28/320500844. So she was blown backward, hit her head, and blacked out..."

"...@k@vS08/31/320900052. The forensics team said that can't be determined from the on-site evidence alone.@k@vS08/31/320900053. But I also think the probability is high.@k@vS08/31/320900054. Even so, still...!"
Fujita tried several times to steady his breathing and calm himself... but in the end he couldn't contain it, and cried out with clenched fists.
"I... I just can't comprehend it!@k@vS08/31/320900056. What reason could she possibly have had to kill her own parents and brother?!"
"And Aoi Hatakeyama is only ten years old!@k@vS08/31/320900058. How could a ten-year-old girl do something so monstrous...?!"
Tomoe remained silent... recalling the Aoi she had met before.
In the park, several of her classmates had hurled vicious words at her over Hinamizawa... even shoving her around.
...It was generally said that group bullying reached its cruelest and most unrestrained between upper elementary school and middle school.
Children that age simply couldn't grasp the concept of restraint, and carried along by mob momentum, they inevitably went too far.
So if Tomoe hadn't happened to be there and stepped in to help, the situation might have been far worse.
...That sorrow transformed into resentment.
When anger sublimated into hatred... it was almost a natural reaction for Aoi to direct that violent emotion and impulse at the family she held responsible...
"...The part I still don't understand is the proactive decision to kill."
"Normally, a child of that age dealing with internal stress would retreat -- shutting themselves in their room, cutting off contact with others. Passive forms of rebellion."
"You're saying the behavior isn't age-appropriate?"

"Right.@k@vS08/28/320500849. ...The fact that she lied in her testimony is also concerning.@k@vS08/28/320500850. And the details were far too polished.@k@vS08/28/320500851. What on earth could have pushed that child's thinking to such an extreme, such maturity...?"
"Minai-san..."

"...@k@vS08/28/320500853. Increase the surveillance on Aoi Hatakeyama.@k@vS08/28/320500854. I have no intention of seeking an arrest warrant at this stage, but we can't rule out the worst-case scenario.@k@vS08/28/320500855. Notify the hospital as well."
"Understood.@k@vS08/31/320900062. I'll get on it..."
"Oh -- Fujita-kun."
Tomoe called out to Fujita as he was about to dash out. After struggling to find the right words, she held up one hand apologetically.
"...Sorry, Fujita-kun. I know this is hard to deal with.@k@vS08/28/320500858. ...I'll treat you to something later."
"Hahaha, it's fine.@k@vS08/31/320900064. I'm your number one errand boy, after all, Minai-san.@k@vS08/31/320900065. ...Just promise me you won't try to shoulder everything alone, okay?"
"Right. ...Thank you."
"...Don't shoulder everything alone, huh."
Having finished the report, Tomoe tossed down her fountain pen, leaned back in her chair, and tilted her chin upward.
On the ceiling, she could see dust-covered fluorescent lights, one of them flickering weakly -- probably on its last legs.
"How do you even process news that only makes people miserable to hear..."
Fujita had said it was 'unthinkable' for a girl that young to commit such violence. ...But Tomoe had been thinking about it from the opposite direction.
(Then what was it that drove her to take action that should have been impossible for her...?)
Murder was generally shunned as an antisocial act. ...But paradoxically, it was precisely because humans possessed social cognition that murder existed.
...In acts of killing not motivated by self-defense or predation, the transition from murderous 'impulse' to 'execution' required a certain level of ego development.
In early childhood, when the ego was not yet formed and one's thoughts and actions were largely dependent on others, one could not find meaning in killing.
One's existence is threatened. -- So they 'kill' the cause.
One's livelihood is imperiled. -- So they 'kill' the source.
Both of those require the cognitive ability to recognize 'existence' and 'livelihood' as things of relative or absolute value -- and that requires a formed sense of self.
And this ability is scarcely developed in young children. It is cultivated through 'dialogue' with others during adolescence, through the construction of social thought.
Therefore, those young enough to be called 'children' did not, as a rule, arrive at the thought or act of killing another person unless by accident.
Because they couldn't comprehend the 'benefit' that killing would bring.
"...But if Aoi Hatakeyama had acquired that 'thought process' --"
Then who had imparted it to her?
Who could have given a mere ten-year-old girl the understanding necessary to carry out such a brutal crime...?
Shaking her head, Tomoe opened her eyes and extracted herself from the labyrinth of thought.
Even she felt she might be overthinking things. ...Still, bizarre as the crimes committed by Hinamizawa survivors were, there had to be some underlying factor.
If they couldn't identify it, the tragedies would simply continue to repeat...
Just then.
"...Oh, there you are.@k@vS08/29/321200194. Sis?"
"Huh?"
"...Oh, it's you, Madoka."
The door to the Investigation Division opened, and in walked her sister, Madoka.
"What brings you here?@k@vS08/28/320500868. It seems awfully early -- were you on the night shift?"
"No, regular day shift.@k@vS08/29/321200196. I heard you were already at work, so I came in a little early."
"Oh, I see."
In truth, she'd been sleeping at Kakiuchi Station overnight, but... Tomoe found it too tedious to explain and didn't bother to correct her.
"...So, what about it?@k@vS08/29/321200198. Are you coming today?"
"Huh, today...?@k@vS08/28/320500871. What are you talking about?"
"Oh, I knew it. She forgot after all."
"I begged you to remember because today is an important day!"
Arms crossed and cheeks puffed, Madoka glared at her. Tomoe hurriedly flipped open her planner to check the schedule.
Right. Today was the day the parents of Chief Yamaoki -- Madoka's fiance -- were coming to town to meet her.
"Well? Does that ring a bell?"
"Ring a bell or not... just go by yourself.@k@vS08/28/320500874. I'm not going to stop you."
She still harbored ill will toward the Chief himself, but she had no grudge against his parents.
They were, admittedly, getting on in years -- calling them 'his parents' made them sound older than 'his son' -- but they had traveled all the way from Yamaguchi to meet their son's fiancee, Madoka.
Tomoe liked to think she wasn't petty enough to spoil an elderly couple's happiness. And when Madoka had brought it up a few days ago, she was sure she'd said, 'Sounds nice.'
(Ah... so that's the 'congratulatory gift' he meant.)
Recalling Ooishi's earlier remark, Tomoe let out an irritated sigh.
"Well, the elderly seem to take a liking to you for some reason.@k@vS08/28/320500877. Just talk normally and it'll go fine.@k@vS08/28/320500878. Keep it relaxed and you'll be all right."
"That's not the point!@k@vS08/29/321200203. You're coming too, right?"
"...Huh?@k@vS08/28/320500880. Wait, you want me to go too?!"
"Obviously!@k@vS08/29/321200205. That's exactly why I came to confirm!"
She hadn't remotely considered that possibility.
...Come to think of it, when she'd told Madoka 'Sounds nice,' her sister had gone home looking suspiciously elated.
(Oh no... is that what she took it to mean...?)
"...So you won't?@k@vS08/29/321200207. Come on, Sis."
"Wait... th-this has nothing to do with me, does it?@k@vS08/28/320500884. Just go on your own."

"What are you talking about?!@k@vS08/29/321200209. It has everything to do with you!@k@vS08/29/321200210. His parents are coming, you know?@k@vS08/29/321200211. If my only family member isn't there, it would be terribly rude!"
This was clearly Tomoe's oversight. And even if it had been an honest mistake, Madoka was entirely in the right. However...
"...I mean,@k@vS08/28/320500887. is that idiot Chief going to be there too?"
"Obviously."
"Then I'm not going."
"Sis!!"
"Ugh, I'm busy!@k@vS08/28/320500890. Tell them the same! Bye!"
Being called 'sister-in-law' by that old man -- absolutely not. ...Tossing those words over her shoulder, Tomoe stood with the report in hand.
...Normally, Madoka would either give up in exasperation or storm off after a heated argument, and that would be the end of it.
But today, it didn't end there.
"W-wait!!"
As Tomoe turned to leave, Madoka seized her arm with both hands and held her fast.
...Madoka gazed up at her, looking as though she might burst into tears at any moment.
"...Please, Sis.@k@vS08/29/321200216. Come with me. Just for today..."
"You're the only family I have, Sis.@k@vS08/29/321200218. I know we fought and I ran away and we lived apart for so long, but..."
"I want to properly introduce myself to Kaoru-san's parents.@k@vS08/29/321200220. And tell them it's because of you, Sis... that I was able to make it this far..."
"...Madoka."

"...I want you to truly accept this, Sis!@k@vS08/29/321200222. I'm serious about this marriage, you know?@k@vS08/29/321200223. It's not like the old days when I made reckless, spur-of-the-moment decisions!@k@vS08/29/321200224. So please!"
"Even if you still can't accept the marriage, I'll keep working hard until you do!@k@vS08/29/321200226. But just for today... just today is all I'm asking -- please cooperate!!"
With an earnestness rare for her, Madoka stared straight into Tomoe's eyes.
Agreeing not to press the issue of the marriage was probably the greatest concession she could make.
...And her insistence that Tomoe 'meet the parents' might mean she was facing serious pressure from their side.
...But if things followed their usual course, Tomoe would flee at this point, they'd fight for a while, and --
A resignation almost akin to despair... was flickering deep in Madoka's eyes.
Seeing that, Tomoe... let her gaze wander several times as though searching for the right words... then said, in a tone of forced nonchalance:
"...Fine.@k@vS08/28/320500896. I'll go.@k@vS08/28/320500897. And... I'll accept the marriage."
"Well, if that's how it is, I'll need to see what kind of people that bastard's parents are.@k@vS08/28/320500899. I'll be watching very carefully to make sure your in-law relationship will work."
"Huh... w-wait... um...@k@vS08/29/321200229. Sis... what did you just...?"
Stunned.
Unable to process those words, Madoka stood frozen, still gripping Tomoe's arm.
...She couldn't believe it -- not immediately.
She'd arranged nineteen separate occasions to plead her case, trying every approach she could think of, and every single one had failed.
So she hadn't even dared to hope that her sister would yield this easily today.
Smiling at her dumbfounded sister, Tomoe gave a shrug. Then, running a hand through her hair to hide her embarrassment, she said:
"I said I'll accept it.@k@vS08/28/320500901. Being the lone holdout just makes me a nuisance.@k@vS08/28/320500902. I'm still only half-convinced it'll work out... but do your best."
"...However! One absolute condition!@k@vS08/28/320500904. That man is never to call me 'sister-in-law.' I will not budge on this.@k@vS08/28/320500905. Understood?"
"...@k@vS08/29/321200232. R-really...?@k@vS08/29/321200233. No takebacks, okay? I won't forgive you if you try.@k@vS08/29/321200234. You're not going to pull an April Fools later, right?!"
"I won't.@k@vS08/28/320500907. What kind of April has heat like this?"
Suddenly embraced, Tomoe let out a yelp of bewilderment and embarrassment.
But... seeing her sister fighting to hold back sobs, she stopped the feet that had been pulling away... and gently stroked the trembling back.
"...Thank you, Sis, Sis...!!"
(This is fine... I think. It's probably time to let go...)
Doubts still lingered, but... strangely, Tomoe felt no regret over what she'd just said.
"...Hehe.@k@vS08/29/321200238. All right then, Sis, I'm counting on you today!"
"Yeah, yeah.@k@vS08/28/320500913. ...By the way, where are you meeting his parents?"
"At the central plaza in front of Kakiuchi Station.@k@vS08/29/321200240. We're meeting there at five, then heading straight to the restaurant."
"Five o'clock, huh.@k@vS08/28/320500915. ...We have a bit of time.@k@vS08/28/320500916. Would it be all right if I stop by the dentist first?@k@vS08/28/320500917. My tooth's been acting up again."
"Sure, but...@k@vS08/29/321200242. wait, you still haven't gotten that taken care of?"
"Sorry about that.@k@vS08/28/320500919. I've been so busy lately, it just kept slipping."
Even so, Tomoe had first mentioned the toothache over a week ago. She could have ducked out during a break to get it seen to, but no -- she was just that earnest about her work.
...Despite her exasperation, Madoka found herself smiling. It was so like her sister.
"How strange.@k@vS08/28/320500921. The pain had died down for a while, so I thought it had healed."
"Cavities don't heal on their own, you know?"
"I know that, I was kidding.@k@vS08/28/320500923. ...Oh, drat.@k@vS08/28/320500924. I left my insurance card at home.@k@vS08/28/320500925. Ugh, where did I put it..."
"Oh, then I'll lend you mine.@k@vS08/29/321200245. Here."

Saying that, Madoka pulled her own insurance card from her wallet and handed it over. ...It was covered in a gaudy floral protective case like a high school girl might use. Tomoe was momentarily at a loss for words.
No, wait... that wasn't the point.
"...What are you saying?@k@vS08/28/320500927. This is your insurance card.@k@vS08/28/320500928. I'm not registered under your plan -- using it would be against the rules."
"Oh come on, don't be so rigid.@k@vS08/29/321200247. It's not like I'm giving it to a stranger. Nobody's going to think it's being misused."
"That's not the point.@k@vS08/28/320500930. We're civil servants -- we need to maintain proper standards and...@k@vS08/28/320500931. ...be... mindful..."
"...What's wrong, Sis?"
Suddenly.
Insurance card still in hand, Tomoe went silent, staring intently at the information printed on it.
Then... she raised her head with a sharp intake of breath, and leaving the bewildered Madoka behind, reached for the telephone and swiftly dialed a number.

"-- Ah, Matsuoka-kun?@k@vS08/28/320500934. ...I don't have time to chat -- can I ask you something quick?@k@vS08/28/320500935. That case... right, that one.@k@vS08/28/320500936. Do you know which hospital the records were recovered from?"
"Sorry Madoka, just a moment.@k@vS08/28/320500938. ...Yes, yes."
Tomoe pressed the receiver to her ear as she pulled a road map from a drawer and spread it open.
She ran her finger along the map to confirm a location, scribbled something on a notepad, then tore off the sheet with a sharp rip and tucked it in.
"Oh, and how's the analysis on that medication coming?@k@vS08/28/320500940. ...I see, understood.@k@vS08/28/320500941. Report to me when it's done.@k@vS08/28/320500942. Thanks."

The moment she hung up, Tomoe strode to the First Division's scheduling board and quickly scrawled her destination: 'Gifu Pref. Police Forensics Div., etc.' Then she whipped around, snatched her jacket from the coat rack, and threw it on.
Her movements were so swift that Madoka stood dumbfounded for a moment...
"W-wait, Sis!@k@vS08/29/321200251. Where are you going?!"
Seeing her sister about to leave without so much as a backward glance, Madoka couldn't help but call out.
...As if only then noticing, Tomoe turned back with an 'Oh--' and scratched her head sheepishly.
"Sorry, something urgent came up.@k@vS08/28/320500944. ...I need to head out for a bit."
"Urgent? ...What about your promise for today?"
"It'll be fine -- I already jotted down the location.@k@vS08/28/320500946. I might be a little late, but... I'll definitely be there.@k@vS08/28/320500947. See you!"
"Hey, come on, Sis!"
"...What's gotten into her."
There had been no time to catch her. ...She was like a whirlwind.
Left with no choice, Madoka placed her trust in her sister -- who always proclaimed that 'breaking promises is what I hate most' -- and headed back to her own workplace to wait...
"...Here are the medical records for Natsumi Kimiyoshi-san and Aoi Hatakeyama-san."
"Thank you for your cooperation."
We each examined the records the head nurse had handed us.
Both patients seemed prone to falling ill at the change of seasons... visiting the hospital irregularly for consultations.
However...
"...This looks normal."
"...Normal indeed."

The fields listing their conditions were written in scrawled German, but the head nurse translated them for us. According to the records, the diagnoses were colds, fevers, and stomachaches -- nothing particularly unusual.
"Can you tell us what their condition was like?"
"Well, I wasn't present during either of their visits, so I'm afraid I don't know anything beyond what's written in the records..."
"However, based on the doctor's assessment and the medications prescribed, I'd say there was nothing that set them apart from typical patients."
"I see.@k@vS08/13/321000688. ...If I may, there's one more thing I'd like to ask.@k@vS08/13/321000689. Does this medication look familiar to you at all?"
"Medicine?@k@vS08/00/VT2B_Cnurse1005. Hmm, I don't recall ever seeing capsules of this color.@k@vS08/00/VT2B_Cnurse1006. Perhaps you could check with the pharmacy on the first floor?"
As I showed her the photograph... I carefully studied the head nurse's expression.
She tilted her head with a puzzled look, as if genuinely seeing it for the first time. ...She didn't appear to be lying.
"...One last question.@k@vS08/11/321100580. Has this hospital ever used any medication with unspecified or unknown drug names and manufacturers?"
"Never.@k@vS08/00/VT2B_Cnurse1008. Not to my knowledge, at least."
The head nurse answered firmly, with a note of displeasure. ...An understandable reaction from someone who took pride in their medical practice.
"Thank you. We're sorry for the disruption.@k@vS08/13/321000692. ...Ooishi-san, shall we try the pharmacy?"
"...In the end, we came up empty."
"A pity, truly.@k@vS08/11/321100582. I had a feeling there was something to be found here."
Stepping out the entrance, Ooishi-san and I turned to gaze up at the towering Saeki General Hospital building.

It was one of the premier general hospitals in the Chubu region, and accordingly, the white structure exuded dignity and... perhaps it was just my jaundiced eye, but a certain haughtiness that seemed to repel outsiders.
...When we went to the pharmacy and showed the pharmacists the photograph of the capsules, their reaction was the same as the head nurse's.
As expected, it hadn't been prescribed at this hospital.
"Even so... if not here, where did the Kimiyoshi and Hatakeyama families obtain this medication?"
"Hmm, a small clinic or doctor's office, perhaps...@k@vS08/11/321100584. Either way, checking every single one would be quite the ordeal."
"...Indeed."

In Kakiuchi City alone, there were easily over a hundred clinics and medical offices of various sizes. Visiting each one to inquire about the medication was utterly impractical in terms of both time and manpower.
"...It can't be helped.@k@vS08/13/321000697. Shall we head back to Kakiuchi Station, Ooishi-san?"
"Yes.@k@vS08/11/321100586. ...What a shame.@k@vS08/11/321100587. I was hoping to bring Tomoe-chan good news and parlay it into a nice dinner on her dime tomorrow.@k@vS08/11/321100588. Nfu fu fu!"
"That's what you were thinking about?@k@vS08/13/321000699. Honestly, you never miss an angle..."
"Oh come now, don't say that.@k@vS08/11/321100590. You're a little curious yourself, aren't you, Akasaka-san~?"
"Wha--?!@k@vS08/13/321000701. I-it's nothing like that! I'm not--"
"Now, now, no need to get defensive.@k@vS08/11/321100592. ...But well, with Madoka-chan getting closer to her finish line, I was hoping Tomoe-chan might find someone nice too."
Saying that, Ooishi-san lit a cigarette and took a deep drag.
...For all his jesting, the gentle look in his eyes resembled that of a father worrying about his daughter.
"You really do care about her, don't you?@k@vS08/13/321000703. Inspector Minai, I mean."

"Hm?@k@vS08/11/321100594. Yes, of course.@k@vS08/11/321100595. A hardworking person like that deserves a commensurate reward -- anything less would be unjust.@k@vS08/11/321100596. Otherwise, I'd have a bone to pick with the Almighty.@k@vS08/11/321100597. Na ha ha."
"Indeed..."
Effort, huh...
My thoughts drifted to Natsumi Kimiyoshi-san.
According to Inspector Minai's inquiries, since moving to Kakiuchi, Natsumi had been under considerable pressure just to keep up with the other students academically.

Transferring from a rural area to a city -- and mid-term enrollment in a college preparatory school at that -- demanded enormous effort and determination on its own. The stress it placed on her must have been anything but ordinary.
Add to that the recent Hinamizawa turmoil... It would hardly be surprising if Natsumi-san were mentally at her limit.

"...She's a diligent girl.@k@vS08/28/320500949. Which is precisely why she bottles everything up, pushes herself too hard, and... I can sense the danger of her breaking down.@k@vS08/28/320500950. ...Please watch over her carefully."
Natsumi Kimiyoshi.
Having left a small town to begin life in an unfamiliar city, she must have been carrying all manner of stress beyond just her studies.
Even adults required considerable time to adjust to changes in their daily rhythm.
...All the more so for a young girl entering the most nerve-wracking period of her life -- preparing for university entrance exams. The mental toll was difficult to overstate.
The patience she had accumulated to overcome all of that rarely showed on the surface, but it was by no means light enough to ignore...
"Something on your mind, Akasaka-san?"
"No...@k@vS08/13/321000706. It's just, as you said, Ooishi-san -- I don't want to live in a world where honest effort goes unrewarded."
"Yes, absolutely."
"...Ah, Wakamura-san?@k@vS08/29/321200256. Thank you for everything. This is Madoka.@k@vS08/29/321200257. Has Kaoru-san left the house yet?"

"Huh? Over two hours ago?@k@vS08/29/321200259. ...That's strange, he doesn't seem to have arrived yet.@k@vS08/29/321200260. ...Right, right.@k@vS08/29/321200261. Understood. I'll wait a bit longer. Thank you."
She hung up the receiver... and let out a heavy sigh.
Wakamura was the housekeeper at Chief Yamaoki's home -- an elderly woman who had managed his household for many years.
When they first met, Wakamura hadn't been particularly welcoming due to Madoka's background... but now they had grown close, with Madoka learning to cook from her and going shopping together.
"...This isn't good."
She had more or less expected Tomoe to be late, but she never imagined Chief Yamaoki would be delayed too.
Thanks to that, she'd been left to entertain two elderly people she had only just met today -- she wanted to complain to someone about it.
That said, she couldn't very well keep his parents waiting indefinitely, so Madoka had taken it upon herself to start the meal. ...But she was running out of ways to stall.
"Well... they seem to be enjoying themselves, so I suppose it's fine."
She'd been anxious about the first meeting, but... Yamaoki's parents turned out to be warm, friendly people with the approachability of country folk.
She had been certain they would scold her for being too young, but neither of them broached the subject at all. Instead, they smiled and nodded as they asked about her hobbies and interests.
(... Kaoru-san must have spoken to them beforehand.)
Sensing that sort of thoughtful groundwork, Madoka felt grateful.
"Even so..."
Madoka couldn't help but let out a small laugh as she gazed outside.
Whether by coincidence or not, this was the very same restaurant where she and Tomoe had reunited and reconciled.
And where she had first confessed about Yamaoki, leading to that fight...
But it hadn't taken long after that to make up, so it was undeniably a place of good fortune.
"...I wonder if we've finally started to reclaim a little of what we lost."
Those happy times that were destroyed in an instant.
And her sister's tireless efforts to fill the void.
...The truth was, she had always wanted to be Tomoe's strength. To cry together, to suffer together... and in the end, to laugh together.
But Tomoe kept sacrificing herself of her own accord, shouldering the burden alone... never willing to rely on Madoka.
So... she decided to leave.
Believing that if she did, her sister might finally turn her attention toward her own happiness...
(... What I did may have been the worst possible choice. But that's all right. In the end, it all worked out.)
Tomoe had risen through the ranks as an elite at the National Police Agency, and Madoka, for better or worse, had found a family.
If she had stayed by her sister's side, they would have kept pushing and enduring... and neither of them would have found much happiness.
She'd earned a great deal of Tomoe's ire for it -- been called an idiot countless times -- but even now, Madoka could say with her head held high that it had been the right thing to do.
After the wedding, she'd want to start a family soon. ...Tomoe would no doubt grumble the whole time, yet secretly look forward to visiting.
She'd heard that Chief Yamaoki's family home had an orchard. ...In a few years he'd be retiring, and helping out there sounded like it could be fun.
And when the seasons changed, she could send Tomoe freshly harvested fruit.
She'd tease her sister -- 'Find yourself someone already' -- while watching with pride as Tomoe continued to flourish...
That was the happiness Madoka envisioned.
A peaceful future, finally within arm's reach...
Just then.
Rough, hurried footsteps echoed from the restaurant entrance.@k There was no need to confirm who it was. The figure rounded the corner of the hallway and appeared before Madoka as she turned.
"Oh, Kaoru-san?@k@vS08/29/321200272. You're late, you know. We've already started eating."
"...Kaoru-san?"
"...@k@vS08/30/321300035. Madoka-kun.@k@vS08/30/321300036. Get ready to leave immediately."
"Huh?"
"...I suppose... she wouldn't come."
"...I'm sorry."
"Th-that can't be true?!"
Ooishi-san shouted those words... then fell literally speechless.
But he was still better off than I was. ...The shock had left me frozen solid -- unable to speak, unable even to breathe.
"...There is... no mistake...!!"
And then.
Detective Fujita -- face drained of color... eyes rimmed in vivid red.
He told us.
That fact.
"Inspector Minai was stabbed at the Kakiuchi service area...@k@vS08/31/320900068. Severe blood loss... she is currently... in critical condition!!"
